commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bundled AICS/Dijie extension, Dijie execution-token protocol schemas, AICS Control UI routes/chat mode, role-builder execution/audit tooling, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +4473, Tests +2497, Docs +813, Other +20. Total +7803 across 43 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new AICS/Dijie bridge rather than reporting a current-main bug. The review path is source and proof inspection of the proposed feature surface.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need product/security review and a narrower branch shape before any automated repair would be safe.

target.title: [AICS-292] Add Dijie AICS execution bridge
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds the local OpenClaw side of the Dijie AICS execution bridge and developer-mode package builder boundary.

- Adds the AICS extension, Dijie execution-token protocol schemas, and local role-builder tooling.
- Enforces developer-mode context isolation so the role builder only receives business logic, public package standards, and an isolated workspace.
- Adds role_package validation that rejects raw execution tokens, cloud bearer values, provider keys, backend-only IDs, order/wallet state, and local absolute paths.
- Adds the AICS UI bridge for marketplace role sync, developer-mode entry, execution-token request, audit readback, and role-builder diagnostics.

Intentionally out of scope: the full human smoke chain in AICS-293, formal cloud account/session bridge, and final developer/user center product surfaces.

Reviewers should focus on the security boundary around developer-mode prompt construction, role_package scanning, and the native/subprocess executor parity.

## Linked context

Related [AICS-291], [AICS-292], [AICS-294], [AICS-293].

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: local Dijie role execution bridge, developer-mode isolation, role package generation validation, and AICS UI entrypoints.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw workspace at `/Users/weizuo/Documents/ai公司/openclaw-base`, running the real control UI mock dev server after this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `npm run dev:ui:mock -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5187` from the OpenClaw repo root.
- Evidence after fix: copied live console output from the local OpenClaw UI mock server:

```text
> openclaw@2026.5.30 dev:ui:mock
> node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-mock-dev.ts --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5187

[control-ui-mock] http://127.0.0.1:5187/chat
```

- Observed result after fix: the patched OpenClaw UI mock server started successfully and exposed the real local chat route at `http://127.0.0.1:5187/chat`, which includes the AICS dashboard/chat surfaces changed by this PR.
- What was not tested: full real-account AICS-293 smoke chain from developer conversation to package upload, purchase, execution, audit upload, and cloud readback.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this PR contains the local bridge and safety boundaries; the full human closed-loop proof depends on the paired Mercur/Dijie cloud PR and later AICS-293 execution.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/aics/index.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/dijie-execution-token.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/dijie.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD`
- `npm run dev:ui:mock -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5187`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- AICS extension tests for developer-mode prompt allowlist/denylist, role_package validation, native/subprocess parity, marketplace role sync, execution-token request, audit readback, and audit upload failure modes.
- Gateway protocol tests for Dijie execution token and role execution schemas.
- UI navigation/browser tests for the AICS page and developer/user mode entry behavior.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The local bridge did not have a tested AICS role-builder package boundary or Dijie execution-token protocol surface.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and run locally.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The AICS extension adds new config paths and local executor behavior.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This PR adds execution-token verification, audit upload/read calls, and role-builder executor boundaries.

What is the highest-risk area?

Developer-mode context isolation and preventing sensitive local/cloud material from entering generated role packages.

How is that risk mitigated?

The role-builder prompt uses an explicit allowlist, input sanitization, isolated workspace execution, minimum package validation, and forbidden-material scanning shared by native and subprocess executors.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review this PR together with the Mercur/Dijie cloud PR, then use AICS-293 for the full human closed-loop smoke.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

External proof for the full closed loop is waiting on AICS-293 after AICS-291/AICS-292/AICS-294 review gates clear.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed initial `dependency-guard` by removing the unnecessary new extension package/lock importer and package export changes; addressed initial `Real behavior proof` by adding live OpenClaw UI mock server output above.
